description:
Advances: none (pure test-suite flakiness fix)

Scope-expansion follow-up from mutagen-wrd.25.3 (S2) — orchestrator-discovered flaky test originating in mutagen-wrd.25.2 (S1).

## What

test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs:135 "find_module_body/2 (mutagen.ast.r2) does NOT call String.to_atom on the target string (r8 invariant)" is order-dependent. It asserts :erlang.system_info(:atom_count) is unchanged before/after a call to Ast.find_module_body/2. In the full suite (make smoke, default seed) it passes; in isolation or under certain test orderings, the atom_count delta is non-zero (observed +2, +3, +66 across runs).

## How it surfaced

During mutagen-wrd.25.3's rebase over the epic branch (which already carried .25.2 + .25.4), the rebase-worker's make smoke with --max-failures 1 happened to pick a seed where this test landed at position #173 and failed. Reproduced the same failure on bare epic/mutagen-wrd HEAD before .25.3 was applied — confirming the flakiness is pre-existing, not regression-induced.

Five subsequent runs with seeds 0, 1, 42, 100, 12345 all green at 608/608.

## Root-cause hypothesis (to verify)

The test's "before" measurement happens AFTER a quoted_module_body/2 helper call that runs Code.string_to_quoted on a source string. If any other test runs concurrently and triggers atom interning between the before_count capture and the after_count capture, the delta reads non-zero.

Possible fixes:
1. Mark the describe block (or the test) async: false if not already
2. Wrap the function-under-test call in a brief lock to ensure no concurrent atom-creation
3. Replace the absolute equality check with a per-call delta that excludes concurrent leakage

Investigation needed to pick the right fix. The r8 invariant the test is checking is real and important — don't weaken the assertion semantics.

## Allowed touches

- test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs
- test/test_helper.exs (if needed for async tagging)
- .spec/specs/ast.spec.md (if the r8 falsifiability claim needs revising)

## Out of Scope

- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex — the production code path is correct; the test is what is wrong
- Weakening or removing the r8 invariant — it is load-bearing for atom-table-DOS prevention
title: fix flaky atom-count test (ast_test.exs r8 invariant order-dependence)
